market_summary: March 2026 developed into a macro-driven risk-off month shaped by conflict-related energy shocks, higher inflation anxiety, and tighter financial conditions. The S&P 500 closed March 20 at 6,506.48, leaving it down 5.0% year to date, while the Nasdaq is down 6.9% year to date, reflecting pressure on long-duration growth exposure. Volatility rose materially from January low-teens into a mid-20s VIX regime after an early-March spike near 29.5, increasing whipsaw and gap risk. The Fed held the policy rate at 3.50-3.75% on March 18 and highlighted elevated uncertainty, while market pricing shifted toward fewer near-term cuts. Sector performance favored energy and defensive real-economy exposures over technology and other rate-sensitive groups.

performance.note: System seed - no live trades yet

strategy_rankings.notes: Expected leader given strong sector rotation into energy/defensives.
Likely useful after volatility spikes in oversold quality names.
Level-based execution should outperform impulsive breakout chasing in this regime.
Intraday VWAP discipline likely valuable in high-noise sessions.
Short-side trend alignment likely stronger than long continuation setups.
Catalyst trading remains relevant but risk must stay smaller due to headline reversals.
Long momentum likely underperformed in unstable macro conditions.
Breakout failures likely increased as volatility and policy uncertainty stayed elevated.

top_lessons: Rate-cut expectations are not a durable edge when oil and inflation shocks are still repricing.
Sector-relative positioning can offer cleaner trades than outright index direction in macro stress.
High-VIX environments reward disciplined exits more than aggressive entries.
Do not extrapolate one-day relief rallies into a regime change without volatility and breadth confirmation.
Cash reserves are a strategic asset during event-driven markets, not idle capital.

strategy_focus: Support/resistance and VWAP execution in choppy sessions
Selective short-trend participation where macro confirms
risk_adjustments: Keep per-trade risk reduced until VIX sustains below 20
Maintain 30-40% cash buffer during unresolved headline risk
Use hard daily stop limits and avoid adding risk late in the session
Scale out of winners earlier in high-volatility conditions
watchlist_themes: Energy leadership durability versus potential de-escalation unwind
Defensive earnings resilience in staples and healthcare
Rate-sensitive technology reaction to 10-year yield changes
Broad-market breadth and volatility compression as regime-shift signals
